# Conversational examples — what people actually say to the dock

> A concrete catalog of **real utterances** a person would say to a desk companion,
> and what they expect back. The point is to LIST first (no model-fitting yet), so we
> can discuss the shapes from real examples rather than abstractions. Each example:
> the **line**, the **expectation**, and the **wrinkle** (what makes it interesting).
>
> Companion: [interaction-models.md](interaction-models.md) (the model taxonomy we'll
> map these onto AFTER discussing them).

---

## A. One-shot Q&A (the baseline)

1. **"What's two plus two?"** → "Four." · *wrinkle:* none — the pure case.
2. **"What's the time?"** → reads it. · *wrinkle:* a trivial built-in tool.
3. **"What's on my calendar today?"** → reads from an integration. · *wrinkle:* a tool
   that does a real (possibly slow) external read; the dock waits, then speaks.
4. **"What am I looking at?" / "Can you see what's on my desk?"** → describes the scene.
   · *wrinkle:* pulls a live vision frame; perception in the turn.
5. **"How do you spell 'necessary'?"** → answers. · *wrinkle:* none, but short — tests
   that quick turns feel instant.

## B. Multi-turn / contextual (needs memory of the exchange)

6. **"What's the weather?" … "And tomorrow?"** → follow-up resolves "tomorrow" against
   the prior turn. · *wrinkle:* the follow-up window (built) + referring back.
7. **"Remember my standup is at 9:30."** … later: **"When's my standup?"** → recalls it.
   · *wrinkle:* persistent memory across turns/sessions, not just this exchange.
8. **"No, I meant the OTHER file."** → repair/correction mid-task. · *wrinkle:* the dock
   must revise, not restart.
9. **"Actually, stop — do this instead."** → interrupt + redirect. · *wrinkle:*
   tap-to-interrupt (built) or voice; then the new instruction supersedes.

## C. Actions / actuation (the dock does something)

10. **"Look up." / "Turn to face me."** → moves the body. · *wrinkle:* fire-and-forget
    motion; instant.
11. **"Do your happy wiggle." / "Dance."** → a canned choreography. · *wrinkle:* a fixed
    motor routine; runs to completion; no feedback.
12. **"Take a photo and send it to me on WhatsApp."** → capture + send. · *wrinkle:* two
    actions, one external + slow; chained.
13. **"Dim the lights and play some jazz."** → two independent actuations in one breath.
    · *wrinkle:* could be parallel; do they both have to succeed?
14. **"Post this to the team Slack: 'shipping at 5'."** → one external action. · *wrinkle:*
    confirmation? does it read back before sending?

## D. Long-running / deferred (outlives the turn)

15. **"Research the best standing desks and tell me what you find."** → "On it; I'll let
    you know." …later… speaks the findings. · *wrinkle:* background task; comes back
    unprompted (built).
16. **"Render this video."** … (minutes pass) … **"Done — it's in your folder."**
    · *wrinkle:* long job + a completion push; maybe progress updates.
17. **"Summarize this 2-hour meeting recording."** → defers, returns a summary. · *wrinkle:*
    long; might want an intermediate "still working, ~40% through."
18. **"Keep an eye on the oven and tell me when 20 minutes is up."** → a timer + a proactive
    nudge. · *wrinkle:* scheduled trigger, not continuous work.

## E. Continuous / sustained behavior (runs WHILE you converse)

19. **"Follow me."** (you walk around) → keeps you in frame / moves with you until "stop."
    · *wrinkle:* the motivating case — a control loop concurrent with conversation.
20. **"Watch the door and tell me if someone comes in."** → idle-watch + a trigger →
    proactive announce. · *wrinkle:* long-lived, low duty cycle, reacts.
21. **"Read me the news while I cook."** (you interject "skip that", "go back") → a long
    monologue you can steer mid-stream. · *wrinkle:* sustained output + barge/steer.
22. **"Keep me company / just chat with me for a while."** → an open-ended, ambient
    presence; occasional unprompted remarks. · *wrinkle:* no task, no goal — a *mode* of
    being around.
23. **"Let me know if I slouch."** → posture-watch over a work session → gentle proactive
    reminders. · *wrinkle:* continuous perception + sparse, well-timed interjections.

## F. Proactive / dock-initiated (no user utterance starts it)

24. (You walk up after a while) → **"Hey, welcome back."** · *wrinkle:* presence-triggered
    greeting; the gate decides whether to speak.
25. (You look stressed on camera) → **"You okay? Want to take a break?"** · *wrinkle:*
    emotion-triggered; high risk of being annoying — the gate must be conservative.
26. (A background task finished) → **"Your render's done."** · *wrinkle:* built — task
    signal → autonomous turn.
27. (Mid-morning) → **"You said to remind you to stretch."** · *wrinkle:* scheduled.

## G. Meta / about-itself (the dock as a configurable thing)

28. **"Speak a bit slower." / "Be more concise."** → adjusts its own style. · *wrinkle:*
    changes a persistent preference, not just this turn.
29. **"What can you do?"** → explains its capabilities. · *wrinkle:* needs an honest
    self-model of its tools/modes.
30. **"Stop listening / go to sleep." / "Wake up."** → toggles its own attention state.
    · *wrinkle:* directly drives the conversation-state machine.
31. **"Forget what I just told you about X."** → edits memory. · *wrinkle:* user-driven
    memory mutation.

## H. Ambiguous / messy (the real world)

32. **"Um… actually never mind."** → user aborts their own request. · *wrinkle:* the dock
    should drop it gracefully, not answer a half-question.
33. **Two people talking; one addresses the dock.** → who is it listening to? · *wrinkle:*
    addressing + identity in a multi-person room.
34. **"Can you…"** (trails off, thinks, continues) → don't fire on the pause. · *wrinkle:*
    endpointing; the VAD/timing knobs.
35. **"Hey — wait, are you recording right now?"** → a question about its live state.
    · *wrinkle:* the dock must introspect what it's currently doing.
